methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $250M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Twelve Labs, a Series A AI & Software company, is valued at $250M post-money after raising $57M. The comparable universe consists of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, comprising eight private companies and one public (Palantir). Top private comparables include Liquid AI, Field AI, and xAI; the peer set ranges from $500M to $32.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $500M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company cohort produces $2.4B, with Liquid AI—a direct Series A peer—valued at $2.4B, suggesting the median is anchored to stage-comparable rather than outlier valuations. The peer set spans $500M to $32.0B, with substantial dispersion driven by downstream funding and performance variance.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The current $250M valuation sits materially below the peer median, consistent with early Series A staging and smaller capital base. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and product differentiation relative to the broader frontier-AI cohort, where valuation dispersion remains acute. The $1.4B median represents a 5.6x step-up from the current valuation, reflecting stage-typical expansion and peer benchmarking.
